title: Subclass 500 Genuine Student requirement - Home Affairs guidance

sections.heading: How Genuine Student is assessed
sections.text: Home Affairs states that the Genuine Student criterion focuses on whether the applicant genuinely intends to study in Australia. The page says the assessment considers the applicant's circumstances, immigration history, compliance with visa conditions, and other relevant matters.

sections.heading: Evidence relevant to Genuine Student
sections.text: The Home Affairs Genuine Student guidance explains that relevant information can include previous study, previous study in Australia, current employment, circumstances in the home country or country of residence, and the value of the course to the applicant's future. It also refers to reasons for not studying in the home country if a similar course is available there.

sections.heading: Why this matters in refusal review or consultation
sections.text: If a Subclass 500 application is refused and the issue relates to Genuine Student concerns, the refusal notice, supporting statement, enrolment details, academic background, employment history, and explanations about study plans may all be important for legal assessment and consultation preparation.
